response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack web developer with 15+ years of broad software experience, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The position has three non-negotiable requirements — native-level Japanese proficiency, strong C programming for embedded UNIX development, and security-domain expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, etc.) — none of which are present in his resume. His technical background is predominantly web-oriented (TypeScript, Node.js, MongoDB), with only superficial mentions of C/C++ and Linux, and no evidence of Linux kernel-level investigation or modification. The language requirement alone is a disqualifying gap.
strengths: 15+ years of diverse software development experience across web, mobile, and embedded contexts
Some C/C++ and Linux exposure across multiple roles, including embedded and low-latency systems work
Strong English communication skills with a track record of international collaboration
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency of any level mentioned — an absolute hard requirement for this role
No experience with relevant security technologies: MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, Captive Portal, MAC authentication, or encryption key exchange protocols
No Linux kernel source-level investigation or modification; no embedded networking, LAN switch, or network hardware experience

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security domain experience
